title: Callista gracilicaulis
taxonomy: Callista gracilicaulis is a genus of sea snails in the family Veneridae. It is classified under the kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, class Bivalvia, order Veneroida, and family Veneridae. The species is further classified into several species, with Callista gracilicaulis being one of them. However, detailed classification of the species within its genus may vary depending on the source. Further research may be necessary to determine the exact classification of Callista gracilicaulis.
uses: Callista gracilicaulis has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the regions where it is native, to treat various health conditions. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and flavonoids in its extracts. The plant has been used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. More research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Traditional healers and local communities continue to rely on Callista gracilicaulis for its perceived medicinal benefits.
